pubmed_links: [Melke et al. (2008) Abnormal melatonin synthesis in autism spectrum disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17505466/)
summary: ASMTL is a pseudoautosomal region 1 (PAR1) gene structurally related to ASMT (the final enzyme in melatonin biosynthesis), arising from an ancient gene fusion event; the closely related ASMT gene has been directly implicated in autism spectrum disorder through a splice site mutation and promoter polymorphisms causing reduced ASMT activity and melatonin levels. ASMTL itself has not been independently studied in neuropsychiatric populations. (novel candidate)
